about.intro: I'm Darren Oommen, a student focused on medicine, neuroscience, biomedical science, and data-driven research.
about.description: I'm a high school student building a profile around healthcare, neuroscience, service, leadership, and research. I am currently learning Python, NumPy, Matplotlib, statistics, and data analysis so I can eventually apply computational tools to biomedical and public health questions.
about.full:
Hi, I'm Darren Oommen. I'm a high school student interested in biomedical science, neuroscience, medicine, and data analysis.

My main academic direction is medicine and neuroscience. I am especially interested in how research, programming, and statistics can be used to better understand health problems. Right now, I am learning Python, NumPy, Matplotlib, and data analysis with the goal of building stronger research-focused projects.

My interest in medicine is also shaped by personal experience. I help care for my brother, who has autism, which has made me more aware of how neurological and developmental conditions affect families and communities.

I'm the co-founder and Healthcare Lead for MyIntern.net, a student internship platform where I focus primarily on healthcare-related opportunities. I am also volunteering at RWJ and Capital Health during summer 2026.

Outside of academics and healthcare, music is a major part of my life. I have performed as a percussionist in youth orchestra, marching band, indoor percussion, church events, weddings, and live community performances. I also started a drum tutoring and summer camp business.

My goal is to combine medicine, neuroscience, research, service, leadership, and creativity into work that has real-world impact.

roadmap.description:
My coursework is centered around STEM, medicine, biomedical science, and research preparation.

Relevant coursework includes Biology through Mercer County Community College, Chemistry Honors, AP Seminar, Pre-Calculus Honors, AP Computer Science Principles, PLTW Introduction to Engineering Design, and dual-enrollment STEM coursework.

I am also self-studying AP Psychology and AP Calculus BC, which connect directly to my interests in neuroscience, medicine, statistics, and computational research.

Freshman year at Mercer County Technical Schools Sypek Center through the MCTS STEM Academy.

Finished freshman year with a 4.44 weighted GPA and a 4.00 unweighted GPA. Took Biology, World History, Spanish I, Algebra II, AP Computer Science Principles, PLTW Introduction to Engineering Design, Physical and Health Education I, and English I.

Dual-enrollment credits came from Mercer County Community College and Rochester Institute of Technology. Courses included MCCC BIO113, MCCC HIS113, MCCC MAT115, MCCC DRA190, and RIT PLTW101.

Scored a 5 on AP Computer Science Principles.

Sophomore year at Hopewell Valley Central High School.

Finished sophomore year with a 4.43 weighted GPA and a 4.08 unweighted GPA. Coursework included AP Seminar, Basic Piano, Chemistry Honors, English 2 Honors, Pre-Calculus Honors, Spanish 3, US History 1 Global Honors, and Wellness 2.

This year helped strengthen my writing, chemistry foundation, math preparation, and research communication skills.

I self-studied AP Psychology and AP Calculus BC to push beyond my school schedule and connect my interests in neuroscience, math, statistics, and biomedical research.

I am also building technical skills in Python, NumPy, Matplotlib, and data analysis. These skills are part of my research direction, not just general coding.

I also earned a Fusion 360 certification, connecting my STEM background to engineering design and digital modeling.

I am interested in biomedical science, neuroscience, medicine, public health, and computational research.

As co-founder and Healthcare Lead for MyIntern.net, I focus primarily on healthcare-related internships and opportunities for students interested in medicine, biomedical science, and related fields.

I am currently volunteering at RWJ and Capital Health during summer 2026. I have also volunteered with special needs children at Princeton Alliance Church during summer Sundays and one-week summer camps in 2024 and 2025.

My experience caring for my brother, who has autism, has made neuroscience and developmental medicine especially meaningful to me.

I serve as Vice President of Model Congress and have been involved in Model United Nations.

At Rutgers Model UN in 2025, I won Best Position Paper in the Disarmament and International Security Committee.

I also served as a Church Lay Leader at Christos Mar Thoma Church in Pennsylvania and managed the choir website during 2024-2025.

Music is a major part of my life, especially percussion and drum set.

I was a GPYO Principal Percussionist for two years and performed at venues including Princeton Richardson Auditorium and Rutgers Mason Gross Performing Arts Center. I marched bass drum in Nottingham Marching Band, snare drum in Nottingham Indoor Percussion, and tenors at Hopewell Valley Central High School.

I have played drum set and percussion at weddings, church events, Main Street Disneyland, and with Musify Philly at the First FOMAA National Women's Summit, where I was featured on Flowers TV.

I also launched a drum tutoring and summer camp business and currently work with one active student.

Outside of academics, medicine, and music, I enjoy creative and hands-on hobbies.

I earned a poom belt in Taekwondo under two-time national champion Master Park. I also enjoy fishing, juggling, origami, and ventriloquism.

I previously created an origami and ventriloquism YouTube channel that reached 52 subscribers, with the highest-viewed video reaching 522 views.
